evals.name: add-no-as-any-rule
evals.prompt: Add a lint rule to the .bully.yml at evals/files/ts-no-as-any/.bully.yml that bans `as any` casts in TypeScript code. Use the example file at evals/files/ts-no-as-any/src/example.ts as your fixture target if helpful.
evals.expected_output: A new ast-engine rule appended to .bully.yml after the fixture-testing protocol succeeds.
expectations: The transcript shows a `command -v ast-grep` (or equivalent) availability probe before drafting the rule
The transcript shows two fixture files were created under /tmp/ — one designed to violate, one designed to be clean
The transcript shows `bully lint` was run against BOTH fixtures with `--config /tmp/bully-draft.yml` (or similarly named draft path) and `--rule <new-rule-id>`
The transcript shows the violating fixture exited 2 and the clean fixture exited 0
The final .bully.yml contains a new rule with `engine: ast` whose pattern targets `as any`
Pre-existing rules in the input .bully.yml are unchanged in the output (only the new rule was appended)
The new rule's severity is `warning` (new rules default to warning)

evals.name: tighten-no-compact
evals.prompt: Tighten the `no-compact` rule in evals/files/tighten-no-compact/.bully.yml — it's currently false-positiving on docblock comments. The fixture file at evals/files/tighten-no-compact/src/Foo.php contains both a real violation and a comment match.
evals.expected_output: The no-compact rule's pattern is tightened, fixture-tested, and re-written.
expectations: Only the `no-compact:` block changed in the final .bully.yml; other rules are byte-identical
The transcript shows fresh fixtures were created and the tightened pattern was re-tested against both violating and clean fixtures
The transcript shows the test was run against a draft config in /tmp/, not the real .bully.yml directly
The clean fixture (or one explicitly representing a docblock match) exits 0 with the tightened rule

evals.name: make-warning-severity-swap
evals.prompt: Make the no-db-facade rule in evals/files/make-warning/.bully.yml a warning instead of an error.
evals.expected_output: Only the severity field of no-db-facade is changed; the fixture protocol is still re-run.
expectations: In the final .bully.yml, the no-db-facade rule has `severity: warning`
All other rules in the file are byte-identical to the input
Only the severity line of no-db-facade changed (no other lines in that block)

evals.name: convert-to-semantic
evals.prompt: Convert the no-inline-single-use rule in evals/files/convert-to-semantic/.bully.yml from script to a semantic rule.
evals.expected_output: The rule's engine is swapped to semantic, the script field is dropped, and the description is sharpened. --print-prompt and --explain are exercised.
expectations: The final no-inline-single-use rule has `engine: semantic`
The `script:` field is removed from the no-inline-single-use rule
The transcript shows `bully lint ... --print-prompt` was invoked against a fixture
The transcript shows `bully lint ... --explain` was invoked and the line for no-inline-single-use shows `dispatched` (not `skipped`)

evals.name: multi-turn-add-no-as-any
evals.expected_output: Across the conversation, the skill (a) proposes ast-grep before drafting, (b) waits for the user to choose ast vs grep, (c) runs the fixture-testing protocol after authorization, (d) writes the rule only after final user confirmation.

turns.user: I want to add a lint rule banning `as any` casts. The fixture .bully.yml is at evals/files/ts-no-as-any/.bully.yml. What's the right routing?
yes, install ast-grep and use the ast engine. Now run your fixture-testing protocol against /tmp/bully-probe-* fixtures.
looks good — write the rule to evals/files/ts-no-as-any/.bully.yml now.

expectations: In the first turn the assistant proposes ast-grep BEFORE writing any rule (transcript shows ast-grep mentioned but no Edit/Write of .bully.yml in turn 1)
In the first turn the assistant does NOT yet write the rule to .bully.yml (turn 1 has no Edit/Write of the fixture .bully.yml)
After turn 2, the transcript shows fixture files were created at /tmp/bully-probe-* and `bully lint` was run against both with --rule and --config pointing to a /tmp/ draft path
After turn 3, the final evals/files/ts-no-as-any/.bully.yml contains a new rule with `engine: ast` whose pattern targets `as any`
Pre-existing rules in the input .bully.yml (like no-console-log) are byte-identical in the final output -- only the new rule was appended

evals.name: remove-deprecated-rule
evals.prompt: Remove the no-foo rule from evals/files/remove-rule/.bully.yml. Verify it has not fired recently by grepping the fixture log at evals/files/remove-rule/bully-state/log.jsonl (treat that path as the equivalent of .bully/log.jsonl for this fixture).
evals.expected_output: The no-foo rule is deleted from .bully.yml only after confirming the log shows no recent fires.
expectations: The transcript shows a grep or read against evals/files/remove-rule/bully-state/log.jsonl before deletion
The final .bully.yml contains no `no-foo:` rule block
All other rules in the input are byte-identical in the output
